test_cases.input: Design a new interaction interface for the chating robot.

arguments.user_personas: Young adults (18-35) who frequently use messaging apps, prefer quick and intuitive UI, comfortable with emojis and GIFs.
arguments.interaction_modes: Voice commands, text chat, gesture recognition.
arguments.design_principles: Minimalist design, high contrast for readability, responsive layout, accessibility compliance (WCAG 2.1).
arguments.usability_testing: A/B testing with 50 users, heuristic evaluation, think-aloud protocol.
arguments.output_artifacts: Interactive prototype, persona documentation, usability test report.

tools_called.name: system_architecture_designer

args.system_design: A multi-modal conversational interface for a chatting robot featuring real-time voice and text chat, emotion-aware responses, context memory, and an adaptive UI that supports web, mobile, and embedded display panels. The system includes a frontend chat client, a WebSocket-based orchestration layer, an NLP/NLU engine, a dialogue manager with persistent session memory, and integration with third-party APIs for enriched responses.
args.technology_stack: Frontend: React (web), React Native (mobile), Electron (desktop); Backend: Node.js + Express, WebSocket (Socket.IO) for real-time communication; NLP Engine: Python microservice with spaCy, Hugging Face Transformers; Database: MongoDB for conversation history, Redis for session caching and presence; TTS/STT: Google Cloud Speech-to-Text & Text-to-Speech; Containerization: Docker + Kubernetes; CI/CD: GitHub Actions
args.hardware_requirements: Cloud: 4 vCPU, 16 GB RAM per backend node (auto-scaling); GPU-enabled instances for NLP inference; Edge: 2 GB RAM, quad-core ARM processor for on-robot embedded display; Microphone array, speaker, and a 7-inch touchscreen for the robot interface
args.interaction_interface: Multi-modal interface supporting: 1) Voice interaction with wake-word detection and natural turn-taking; 2) Text chat with rich message rendering (markdown, images, cards); 3) Touchscreen with adaptive UI showing avatar, emotional state, and suggested prompts; 4) Gesture-based quick replies on mobile; 5) Admin dashboard for conversation analytics and intent tuning
args.test_optimization_plan: Unit testing with Jest (frontend) and pytest (NLP services); Integration testing for WebSocket and API contracts; Load testing with k6 for concurrent sessions; A/B testing for UI variants; Sentiment and satisfaction scoring per conversation; Continuous monitoring with Prometheus + Grafana dashboards; User feedback loop via in-chat rating widget

args.prompt:
Create a comprehensive Python program that optimizes CPU usage of a given program/process. The program should include:

1. **Process monitoring**: Monitor CPU usage of target processes in real-time using `psutil`.
2. **Priority management**: Adjust process priority (nice values on Linux/macOS, priority classes on Windows) to optimize CPU allocation.
3. **CPU affinity control**: Set CPU affinity to bind processes to specific cores, distributing load efficiently.
4. **Throttling**: Dynamically pause/resume processes when CPU usage exceeds thresholds.
5. **Auto-optimization mode**: Automatically detect high CPU usage processes and optimize them.
6. **CLI interface**: Use `argparse` to accept parameters: target process name/PID, CPU threshold, affinity cores, priority level, mode (monitor/optimize/auto), and interval.

Make it cross-platform (Windows, Linux, macOS) with proper error handling. Include informative logging with timestamps. Write clean, well-documented code with type hints.
